page: OVHcloud r2-240 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 240.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory performance presents a similar tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The high memory density relative to compute resources makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require cutting-edge single-thread CPU speeds. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform in the average tier. Conversely, tasks demanding high-throughput text generation or large language model inference are poorly supported, with 7B model text generation falling into the bottom 10%. The server is best suited for in-memory caching, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: OVHcloud r2-240 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 2.2 GHz. It is configured with 240.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 400 GB local SSD, and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface. While single-core CPU benchmarks and uncached large-block memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 10% to 25% ranges, multi-core performance and database operations consistently reach average tiers. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that prioritize high RAM capacity over raw single-threaded compute power. It is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded processing workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-240 is a memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel cores, 240 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, delivering average multi-core and database performance alongside weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: OVHcloud r2-240 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel cores, 240 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory configuration with dedicated cores and local SSD storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel physical cores running at 2.2 GHz
240 GB of RAM providing 15 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Average multi-core, database, and Redis performance
Weak single-core and uncached memory bandwidth performance
